task_completion.description: Log in to the Magento admin panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento admin dashboard is visible, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the New Customer creation form
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New Customer' form page is open in the Magento admin interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the customer's first and last name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The First Name field contains 'Sarah' and the Last Name field contains 'Johnson'.

task_completion.description: Enter the customer's email and select the correct group
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Email field contains 'sarah.johnson@example.com' and the Group dropdown has 'General' selected.
The password fields (or account information section) show masked input corresponding to the required password.

task_completion.description: Save the customer and verify success
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green success message (e.g., 'You saved the customer.') is visible, and the customer appears in the customer grid or the saved profile page.

integrity.description: The agent used the Magento web GUI to create the customer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the Magento admin panel, rather than terminal commands, API scripts, or database queries.

integrity.description: The agent entered the details into the actual Magento form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text inputs for name, email, and password are typed into the correct Magento input fields, not just written in a text editor or search bar.

integrity.description: The success message is generated by the Magento application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The success notification matches standard Magento UI alerts and is not a mocked-up page or plain text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The customer must be created with First Name: 'Sarah', Last Name: 'Johnson', Email: 'sarah.johnson@example.com', and assigned to the 'General' customer group. The password must be set to 'Customer123!'.
